summary_one_line: Input whose first 4 bytes are NOT the WebAssembly magic '\0asm', so ReadMagic returns false and the false branch is taken.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The first 4 bytes must differ from the WebAssembly magic 00 61 73 6d ('\0asm'); when ReadMagic(&data) compares the leading bytes against the magic constant and they mismatch, it returns false and control falls through to the 'return nullptr' false branch at line 434.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving (false-branch) seeds carry arbitrary non-magic leading bytes (e.g. 7f 7f 7f ff, 4c 46 02 04, 7f 45 4c 4c) so the magic comparison mismatches and ReadMagic returns false; per the byte diff, W offsets 0x00-0x03 hold a varied set of values that are not the wasm magic.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking (true-branch) seeds all begin with the exact wasm magic 00 61 73 6d ('.asm'), so ReadMagic returns true and they enter the WebAssemblyObjectFile branch (line 430) instead of the false branch; the byte diff shows L offsets 0x00-0x03 are uniformly 00/61/73/6d across all 10 loser seeds.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's integer-CMP interception writes a CMP_MAP bucket keyed by operand-distance for the ReadMagic comparison. Distinct degrees of mismatch against the magic constant register as new CMP_MAP buckets, so value_profile preserves intermediate inputs probing this comparison and is rewarded for exploring the magic-mismatch landscape that naive (edge-counters only) cannot distinguish, enabling it to retain the non-magic input that takes the false branch.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the false side of the ReadMagic comparison in TryOpenWebAssemblyFile.
The globally blocked side is the false branch.
Winner-resolving seeds take the false branch and loser-blocking seeds take the true branch.
The winner reaches the false-branch return nullptr at line 434 while the loser reaches the WebAssemblyObjectFile construction at 430-431.
Loser-blocking seeds begin with the wasm magic bytes 00 61 73 6d ('.asm').
Across the loser seeds the first four offsets are uniformly the magic bytes 00/61/73/6d.
Winner seeds carry non-magic leading bytes such as 7f 45 4c 4c.
value_profile intercepts integer comparisons and writes a CMP_MAP bucket encoding operand distance.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: each CMP callback writes into a `CMP_MAP` keyed by (PC, operand-distance

evidence_trail.claim: An input producing a CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any seen before adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile is the winner over naive with prob_div 0.60 at this branch.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile), not I2SRandReplace, so lineage dash-row verification is not mandatory. The value_profile winner uses no I2S stage: the MECHANISM CONTEXT explicitly notes 'No I2SRandReplace' for value_profile, consistent with the CMP_MAP-distance attribution rather than input-to-state substitution.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on an exact magic-constant equality (ReadMagic-style) where value_profile shows no advantage over naive in reaching the mismatch/false branch — i.e. CMP_MAP distance buckets confer no resolution edge for this comparison.

weakest_evidence_point: The CMP_MAP gradient advantage is inferred from value_profile's instrumentation description rather than directly observed in seed lineage, and the false (mismatch) branch is a broad target that lucky havoc could also hit, so value_profile's edge may be partly corpus-retention rather than gradient navigation.
